name: Dr Christopher Wolfe
focus: Mars atmosphere dynamics • Surface-atmosphere interactions • Thermal infrared modeling
bio: Chris A. Wolfe is a planetary science researcher at Northern Arizona University working within the Planetary Instrumentation eXperimentation and Exploration Laboratory (PIXEL). His research focuses on the study of planetary atmospheres and surface–atmosphere interactions, particularly on Mars. He specializes in using thermal infrared data and numerical modeling to investigate dust dynamics, including how dust is lifted, transported, and deposited across the Martian surface. His work has contributed to studies using data from instruments such as the Emirates Mars Infrared Spectrometer (EMIRS), helping to quantify changes in surface dust thickness and better understand Martian climate processes. Through integrating remote sensing observations and modeling techniques, Wolfe’s research advances knowledge of atmospheric behavior and surface evolution on Mars.
